Title: Create an encounter using each configured encounter type and verify type-specific defaults
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; multiple encounter types configured (Vitals, Consultation, Admission, Discharge); patient with an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with an active visit | 2. Start a new encounter | 3. Select encounter type Consultation | 4. Save | 5. Repeat for Vitals, Admission and Discharge types
Expected_Result: Each encounter is saved with the selected encounter type, the correct default form binding and type-appropriate role prompts

Title: Encounter cannot be saved without an encounter type
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new encounter | 2. Leave encounter type unset | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked and a validation message indicates encounter type is required

Title: Encounter type list is filtered by the user's privileges
Preconditions: Two roles: Nurse (Vitals only) and Physician (all types); both can log in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as Nurse | 2. Open new encounter and inspect the encounter type list | 3. Log out and log in as Physician | 4. Open new encounter and inspect the list
Expected_Result: Nurse sees only privileged encounter types; Physician sees the full set; no privileged type is selectable by Nurse

Title: Enter a multi-section consultation form and save all sections in one encounter
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; multi-section HTML form (History, Exam, Plan) bound to Consultation; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient with an active visit | 2. Start the Consultation form | 3. Complete History section | 4. Complete Exam section | 5. Complete Plan section | 6. Save the encounter
Expected_Result: A single encounter is saved containing observations from all three sections with correct grouping

Title: Form-level required-field validation blocks save when a required observation is empty
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form with at least one required obs field; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the clinical form | 2. Leave a required field empty | 3. Complete all other fields | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Save is prevented, the required field is highlighted and an inline error indicates the field is required

Title: Numeric vitals field rejects values outside the configured absolute range
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; vitals form with pulse range 0-300; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the vitals form | 2. Enter Pulse=350 | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked and a range validation error is shown; the obs is not persisted

Title: Numeric vitals field accepts the exact lower and upper boundary values
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; vitals form with pulse absolute range 0-300; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the vitals form | 2. Enter Pulse=0 and save | 3. Reopen form, enter Pulse=300 and save
Expected_Result: Both boundary values are accepted and persisted as valid observations

Title: Numeric field warns but allows values outside critical range yet within absolute range
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; vitals form with pulse critical 40-150, absolute 0-300; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Pulse=35 (below critical, within absolute) and save | 2. Enter Pulse=200 (above critical, within absolute) and save | 3. Enter Pulse=400 (above absolute) and save
Expected_Result: 35 and 200 save with a high/low warning; 400 is rejected as a hard error

Title: Numeric field rejects non-numeric and malformed input
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; vitals form; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Pulse=abc | 2. Attempt save | 3. Enter Pulse=7.2.3 | 4. Attempt save | 5. Enter Pulse=72,5 | 6. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Each malformed entry is rejected with a numeric format error and no obs is saved

Title: Numeric precision is enforced for fields configured to whole numbers
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; respiratory rate field configured as integer; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter RespiratoryRate=18.5 | 2. Attempt save
Expected_Result: System either rejects the decimal with a precision error or rounds per configuration consistently; behavior is deterministic

Title: Select a coded answer from a defined answer set and verify the concept is stored
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; coded obs field (e.g., Chief Complaint) with answer concept set; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Open the coded field dropdown | 3. Select a valid coded answer | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The selected concept is stored as the obs value coded and renders correctly on the encounter view

Title: Coded field rejects a free-text value not present in the answer set
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; strict coded field with closed answer set; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Type a value not in the answer set into the coded field | 3. Attempt save
Expected_Result: The entry is not accepted; only answer-set concepts can be selected and saved

Title: Autocomplete coded field returns only concepts within the configured answer set
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; autocomplete-style coded field; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Type partial text in the autocomplete coded field | 3. Inspect suggestions
Expected_Result: Only concepts belonging to the field's answer set are suggested; out-of-set concepts do not appear

Title: Multi-select coded field stores multiple obs for one concept question
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; checkbox/multi-select coded field; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Select two or more coded answers in the multi-select field | 3. Save | 4. Reopen encounter
Expected_Result: Each selected answer is stored as a distinct obs and all reappear on reopen

Title: Create a repeating obs group and add multiple member rows
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form with a repeating obs group (e.g., medication line items); active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Add first obs group row and complete members | 3. Click add to create a second row | 4. Complete the second row | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Two obs group instances are saved, each with its own member obs correctly nested under the group concept

Title: Remove an obs group row before save and confirm it is not persisted
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; repeating obs group form; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Add two obs group rows | 2. Complete both | 3. Remove the second row | 4. Save | 5. Reopen encounter
Expected_Result: Only the remaining obs group instance is persisted; the removed row leaves no orphan obs

Title: Partially completed obs group enforces member-level required rules
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; obs group where member dose is required when drug is set; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Add an obs group row | 2. Set the drug member | 3. Leave the required dose member empty | 4. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a member-level validation error scoped to that group row

Title: Obs group nesting hierarchy is preserved through save and reload
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; nested obs group form; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Save an encounter containing a nested obs group | 2. Query obs table for the encounter | 3. Verify obs_group_id linkage of members to parent
Expected_Result: Member obs rows reference the parent grouping obs via obs_group_id; hierarchy matches the form structure

Title: Edit an existing encounter observation and verify the value updates
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with edit privilege; saved encounter with editable obs; visit not closed
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter | 2. Click Edit | 3. Change an obs value | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The encounter reflects the updated value; the prior obs is voided and a new obs recorded per versioning rules

Title: Editing an encounter creates an audit trail of the change
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with edit privilege; saved encounter; audit/history enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Edit an obs value in an encounter and save | 2. Open encounter history/audit | 3. Inspect changed-by, datetime and prior value
Expected_Result: An audit entry records who changed the obs, when, and retains the voided prior value for traceability

Title: User without edit privilege cannot modify a saved encounter
Preconditions: User with view-only encounter privilege; saved encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as view-only user | 2. Open the encounter | 3. Look for and attempt Edit
Expected_Result: Edit action is hidden or disabled; any direct edit attempt is denied with an authorization error

Title: Void an encounter with a void reason and confirm it is removed from active views
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with void privilege; saved non-voided encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter | 2. Choose Delete/Void | 3. Enter a void reason | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Encounter transitions to voided state, disappears from the active encounters list, and is excluded from clinical summaries

Title: Voiding an encounter requires a non-empty void reason
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with void privilege; saved encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter | 2. Choose Void | 3. Leave the void reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Void is blocked and a validation message requires a void reason before proceeding

Title: Voiding an encounter cascades to void its child observations
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with void privilege; encounter with multiple obs; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Void an encounter with several obs | 2. Query obs rows for that encounter | 3. Inspect voided flag and void reason on members
Expected_Result: All child obs are marked voided with a void reason and voided_by/date set; none remain active

Title: Unvoid (restore) a previously voided encounter where supported
Preconditions: Admin/privileged user; a voided encounter exists; unvoid capability enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Locate the voided encounter via admin tooling | 2. Trigger unvoid/restore | 3. Verify it returns to active views
Expected_Result: The encounter and its obs return to active state; audit records the unvoid action and actor

Title: Assign a provider to a specific encounter role and verify storage
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; provider directory populated; encounter roles configured (Clinician, Consultant); active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start an encounter | 2. Assign Provider A to role Clinician | 3. Assign Provider B to role Consultant | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Both provider-role assignments are stored on the encounter and shown distinctly on the encounter view

Title: Multiple providers can be attached to the same encounter role
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; encounter roles allow multiple providers; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start an encounter | 2. Add Provider A and Provider B both under role Clinician | 3. Save | 4. Reopen
Expected_Result: Both providers persist under the same role and reappear on reopen

Title: Encounter requiring a primary provider blocks save when none is set
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; encounter type configured to require a provider; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the encounter | 2. Leave all provider roles empty | 3. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation message that at least one provider is required

Title: Remove a provider-role assignment from an existing encounter
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with edit privilege; encounter with two provider-role assignments
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the encounter | 2. Remove the Consultant provider | 3. Save | 4. Reopen
Expected_Result: The removed assignment no longer appears; remaining assignment is intact; change is audited

Title: Record a retrospective encounter with a backdated encounter datetime within the visit window
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with retrospective entry privilege; visit spanning a past date range
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Start a retrospective encounter | 3. Set encounter datetime to a past date inside the visit | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The encounter is saved with the backdated datetime and is associated with the correct visit

Title: Backdated encounter datetime cannot precede the visit start
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; visit with a defined start datetime
Test_Steps: 1. Start a retrospective encounter | 2. Set encounter datetime before the visit start | 3. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation message that the encounter must fall within the visit window

Title: Encounter datetime cannot be set in the future
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start an encounter | 2. Set encounter datetime to tomorrow | 3. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a future-datetime validation error and the encounter is not persisted

Title: User lacking retrospective privilege cannot backdate an encounter
Preconditions: User without retrospective entry privilege; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as non-privileged clinician | 2. Start an encounter | 3. Attempt to change the datetime to the past
Expected_Result: Datetime is locked to now or backdating is rejected with an authorization message

Title: Retrospective entry binds encounter to the correct visit when multiple past visits exist
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient with two non-overlapping past visits
Test_Steps: 1. Start a retrospective encounter | 2. Set datetime inside visit B window | 3. Save | 4. Verify visit association
Expected_Result: The encounter is associated with visit B (the visit whose window contains the datetime), not visit A

Title: Conditional (skip-logic) field appears only when its trigger answer is selected
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form with conditional field shown when Pregnant=Yes; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Verify the conditional gestational-age field is hidden | 3. Set Pregnant=Yes | 4. Verify the field appears
Expected_Result: The conditional field is hidden by default and revealed only when the trigger answer is selected

Title: Hidden conditional field does not persist a value when its trigger is not met
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; conditional field as above; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Set Pregnant=Yes and enter gestational age | 2. Change Pregnant=No (field hides) | 3. Save | 4. Reopen encounter
Test_Data: Pregnant Yes->No; GestAge entered then hidden
Expected_Result: The previously entered gestational age is not persisted while the trigger condition is unmet

Title: Cross-field validation enforces logical consistency (e.g., systolic greater than diastolic)
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; vitals form with BP cross-field rule; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Systolic=80 Diastolic=120 | 2. Attempt save | 3. Enter Systolic=120 Diastolic=80 | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Inverted BP is rejected with a cross-field error; valid BP is accepted

Title: Date observation field rejects an impossible calendar date
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form with a date obs field; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter date 2026-02-30 in a date obs field | 2. Attempt save
Expected_Result: The invalid calendar date is rejected with a date-format/validity error

Title: Free-text obs field enforces maximum length boundary
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; free-text obs with max length 255; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter 255 characters and save | 2. Enter 256 characters and attempt save
Expected_Result: 255 characters save successfully; 256 characters are truncated or rejected with a length message consistently

Title: Free-text obs field neutralizes script injection input
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; free-text obs field; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a script payload into a free-text obs | 2. Save | 3. Reopen the encounter view
Expected_Result: The value is stored as inert text and rendered escaped on the encounter view; no script executes

Title: Coded answer concept id cannot be tampered to an out-of-set concept via request manipulation
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; intercepting proxy; coded field with closed answer set
Test_Steps: 1. Begin a form submission | 2. Intercept the request and replace the coded concept id with an out-of-set concept | 3. Forward the request
Expected_Result: The server rejects the submission or ignores the invalid concept; no out-of-set value is persisted

Title: Encounter cannot be created against a patient the user is not authorized to access
Preconditions: User restricted to a subset of patients/locations; a patient outside that scope exists
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the restricted user | 2. Attempt to create an encounter referencing the out-of-scope patient via UI/API
Expected_Result: Creation is denied with an authorization error and no encounter is written

Title: Create an encounter with observations via REST and verify the persisted resource
Preconditions: Valid REST credentials; patient uuid and active visit uuid known; encounter type uuid known
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/encounter with encounterType, patient, visit, encounterDatetime and obs array | 2. Capture response | 3. GET the created encounter
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 with an encounter uuid; GET returns the encounter with the submitted obs and provider data

Title: REST encounter create rejects a missing required encounterType
Preconditions: Valid REST credentials; patient uuid known
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/encounter omitting encounterType | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation error naming encounterType; no encounter is created

Title: REST encounter create with an obs value outside the concept range is rejected
Preconditions: Valid REST credentials; numeric concept with defined absolute range
Test_Steps: 1. POST an encounter with a numeric obs above the concept absolute high | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: The API rejects the out-of-range obs with a validation error and persists nothing

Title: Void an encounter via REST DELETE with a reason and verify state
Preconditions: Valid REST credentials with void privilege; existing encounter uuid
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/encounter/{uuid}?reason=test | 2. GET the encounter with voided included
Expected_Result: The encounter is marked voided with the supplied reason; default GET no longer returns it as active

Title: Retrieve an encounter as a FHIR Encounter resource and validate required elements
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; an existing encounter mapped to FHIR
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Encounter/{id} | 2. Validate resourceType, status, class, subject and period
Expected_Result: A valid FHIR R4 Encounter is returned with status, class, subject reference and period populated

Title: FHIR Encounter status maps correctly across the encounter lifecycle
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; encounters in in-progress and finished states
Test_Steps: 1. GET a FHIR Encounter for an open visit encounter | 2. GET a FHIR Encounter after the visit is closed | 3. Compare status codes
Expected_Result: Status reflects the lifecycle (e.g., in-progress for active, finished for closed) per the mapping

Title: Form observations are retrievable as FHIR Observation resources linked to the Encounter
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; encounter with obs created via a form
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Observation?encounter={encounterId} | 2. Validate each Observation has code, value and encounter reference
Expected_Result: Observations are returned with LOINC/SNOMED-style coding where mapped, values, and a reference back to the Encounter

Title: Obs group maps to a FHIR Observation with hasMember components
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; encounter containing a nested obs group
Test_Steps: 1. GET the grouping Observation | 2. Inspect hasMember/component references to member observations
Expected_Result: The grouping Observation references its member Observations, preserving the obs group hierarchy

Title: Inbound HL7 ORU/observation message creates an encounter with observations
Preconditions: HL7 inbound channel configured; valid patient identifier mapping
Test_Steps: 1. Submit an HL7 message with PV1 (visit), OBR and OBX segments | 2. Process the message | 3. Verify the resulting encounter and obs
Expected_Result: An encounter is created with obs mapped from OBX segments and the provider mapped from the message

Title: HL7 message with an unmappable observation code is queued/errored rather than silently dropped
Preconditions: HL7 inbound channel configured; error queue available
Test_Steps: 1. Submit an HL7 message containing an OBX code with no concept mapping | 2. Process | 3. Inspect the error queue
Expected_Result: The message is flagged to the error queue with a clear reason; no partial/silent obs loss occurs

Title: Saved encounter persists encounter_datetime, type, location and creator correctly
Preconditions: DB read access; an encounter just saved via the UI
Test_Steps: 1. Save an encounter | 2. Query the encounter table by uuid | 3. Verify encounter_type, encounter_datetime, location_id, creator and date_created
Expected_Result: All key columns match the UI inputs; creator and date_created reflect the acting user and save time

Title: Encounter providers are stored in encounter_provider with correct role linkage
Preconditions: DB read access; encounter with two provider-role assignments
Test_Steps: 1. Query encounter_provider for the encounter | 2. Verify provider_id and encounter_role_id per assignment
Expected_Result: Each provider-role pair has a row with the correct role id and is not voided

Title: Form-saved obs reference the originating form and encounter
Preconditions: DB read access; obs saved via a specific HTML form
Test_Steps: 1. Save an encounter via a form | 2. Query obs for the encounter | 3. Verify encounter_id and form linkage
Expected_Result: Obs rows reference the encounter; the encounter references the form used for entry

Title: Clinical form fields are reachable and operable via keyboard only
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; a multi-field clinical form; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Open the form | 2. Tab through every field in logical order | 3. Operate dropdowns, date pickers and checkboxes via keyboard | 4. Save via keyboard
Expected_Result: Every control is reachable in a logical tab order and fully operable without a mouse; save succeeds

Title: Form validation errors are announced to assistive technology
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; screen reader active; form with required fields
Test_Steps: 1. Submit the form with a required field empty | 2. Observe the screen reader output for the error
Expected_Result: The validation error is programmatically associated with the field and announced; focus moves to the first error

Title: Coded field labels and required state are exposed via accessible name and ARIA
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; screen reader/inspector; form with coded and required fields
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect each field for an accessible name | 2. Verify required fields expose aria-required | 3. Verify error messaging uses aria-describedby
Expected_Result: Each field has a programmatic label, required fields are marked aria-required, and errors are linked via aria-describedby

Title: Concurrent edit of the same encounter by two users is handled without silent data loss
Preconditions: Two clinicians with edit privilege; same saved encounter open in two sessions
Test_Steps: 1. User A and User B both open the encounter for edit | 2. User A changes an obs and saves | 3. User B changes a different obs and saves
Test_Data: Concurrent edit by A and B
Expected_Result: The second save either merges safely or warns of a stale version; no change is silently overwritten/lost

Title: Unsaved form changes prompt a confirmation before navigation away
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form with unsaved edits
Test_Steps: 1. Enter data into a form without saving | 2. Attempt to navigate away or close the tab
Expected_Result: A confirmation prompt warns about unsaved changes before discarding them

Title: Double-clicking Save does not create duplicate encounters
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; clinical form completed; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Complete the form | 2. Rapidly click Save twice | 3. Check the recent encounters list
Expected_Result: Exactly one encounter is created; the duplicate submission is prevented or de-duplicated

Title: Encounter creation across encounter-type, provider-role and entry-mode combinations
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; multiple encounter types, roles and entry modes (live, retrospective) available
Test_Steps: 1. Create encounters covering pairwise combinations of {type: Vitals,Consultation} x {role: Clinician,Consultant} x {mode: live,retrospective} | 2. Save each | 3. Verify persistence
Test_Data: Pairwise: type x role x mode
Expected_Result: Each combination saves correctly with the expected type, provider role and datetime semantics

Title: Form field validation across data-type and requiredness combinations
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form with numeric/coded/text/date fields each optionally required
Test_Steps: 1. Exercise pairwise combinations of {data type} x {required,optional} x {valid,invalid input} | 2. Attempt save for each
Test_Data: Pairwise: datatype x required x validity
Expected_Result: Valid+required and valid+optional save; invalid inputs are rejected with type-appropriate errors regardless of requiredness

Title: Exploratory session on form rendering across browsers and zoom levels
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; representative complex multi-section form; multiple browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Open the form in Chrome, Firefox and Safari | 2. Resize and zoom to 200 percent | 3. Interact with all controls and obs groups | 4. Note rendering or interaction defects
Expected_Result: Form remains usable and legible across browsers and zoom; defects are logged with reproduction steps

Title: Exploratory probing of obs group add/remove churn for orphaned data
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; repeating obs group form
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly add and remove obs group rows in varied orders | 2. Partially fill some rows | 3. Save and reopen | 4. Inspect for orphaned or duplicated obs
Expected_Result: No orphaned, duplicated or ghost obs persist; saved data matches the final visible form state

Title: Editing a voided encounter is not permitted
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with edit privilege; a voided encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Locate a voided encounter | 2. Attempt to open it for edit
Expected_Result: Edit is not available for voided encounters; the user must unvoid first where supported

Title: Changing an existing encounter's type is restricted or audited
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with edit privilege; saved encounter of type Consultation
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the encounter | 2. Attempt to change encounter type to Admission | 3. Save | 4. Review audit
Expected_Result: Type change is either blocked or permitted only with privilege and is recorded in the audit trail

Title: Required obs becomes non-required when its section is skipped via skip-logic
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form where a section is conditionally shown and its fields required only when visible
Test_Steps: 1. Keep the conditional section hidden and save | 2. Reveal the section, leave its required field empty and save
Test_Data: Section hidden vs shown; required field empty
Expected_Result: Hidden-section required field does not block save; once the section is shown the field is enforced as required

Title: Retrospective encounter without an enclosing visit is handled per configuration
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; retrospective entry to a date with no existing visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start a retrospective encounter on a date with no visit | 2. Save | 3. Inspect whether a visit is auto-created or the save is blocked
Expected_Result: Behavior matches configuration: either an enclosing visit is auto-created or the save is blocked with guidance; never an orphan encounter

Title: Provider list excludes retired providers from selection
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; at least one retired provider in the directory; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start an encounter | 2. Open the provider selector | 3. Search for the retired provider
Expected_Result: Retired providers are not selectable for new encounter-role assignments

Title: Saving a form with no observations entered is rejected when at least one obs is required
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; form requiring at least one observation; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start the form | 2. Enter nothing | 3. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a message that at least one observation is required; no empty encounter is created

Title: Re-saving an edited form preserves untouched observations unchanged
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with edit privilege; encounter with several obs
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the encounter | 2. Change only one obs | 3. Save | 4. Verify other obs are unchanged and not re-voided unnecessarily
Expected_Result: Only the changed obs is versioned; untouched obs retain their original obs id and values

Title: FHIR Encounter participant maps the encounter provider and role
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; encounter with a provider-role assignment
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Encounter | 2. Inspect participant.individual and participant.type
Expected_Result: The provider is represented in participant.individual with a role mapping in participant.type

Title: Encounter datetime in the future is rejected by the REST API
Preconditions: Valid REST credentials; patient and visit uuids known
Test_Steps: 1. POST an encounter with encounterDatetime set to a future timestamp | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 with a validation error about future datetime; no encounter created

Title: All encounter create, edit and void actions are captured for compliance review
Preconditions: Auditing enabled; clinician performs a create, an edit and a void
Test_Steps: 1. Create an encounter | 2. Edit an obs | 3. Void the encounter | 4. Open the audit/compliance log | 5. Verify each action with actor, timestamp and target
Expected_Result: Each action is recorded with acting user, timestamp and encounter reference, sufficient for a compliance audit

Title: Encounter and obs data are not exposed to unauthenticated requests
Preconditions: No active session; a known encounter uuid
Test_Steps: 1. Without authenticating, request the encounter via REST and FHIR endpoints | 2. Inspect responses
Expected_Result: Both endpoints return 401/403 with no PHI in the body; encounter data is not disclosed

Title: Session timeout during form entry does not silently discard data without notice
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; short session timeout configured; form partially filled
Test_Steps: 1. Begin filling a form | 2. Allow the session to time out | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: On save the user is informed of the expired session and prompted to re-authenticate; no silent data loss without warning

Title: Voided encounter is excluded from clinical summary aggregations
Preconditions: DB read access; patient with one active and one voided encounter of the same type
Test_Steps: 1. Void one encounter | 2. Run the clinical summary/aggregation query for the patient | 3. Verify counts
Expected_Result: Aggregations count only the active encounter; voided records are excluded from clinical summaries
